fields.description:
# Cupid enrichment op: LLM ticket-digest extractor + digest schema

## Why
Semantic overlap detection needs a compact, normalized per-ticket representation that catches paraphrase ("login broken" vs "users can't authenticate") WITHOUT embeddings, keeping rebar on its Anthropic-only, no-heavy-ML grain. The Cupid pattern (arXiv:2308.10022) uses the LLM as a one-time feature extractor. DERISKED: the recall spike (see epic experiment comment) showed digest-BM25F retrieves 8/8 heavy paraphrases at rank 1 (recall@20 = 1.0), so this digest is the load-bearing artifact of the whole feature.

## What
A new LLM operation `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` returning a validated dedup digest, plus its output schema and prompt. Modeled on `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (assemble context â†’ load prompt â†’ `RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")` â†’ validate) â€” NOT on `operations.py::review_ticket`, which uses `mode="findings"`/`execution_mode="agentic"` (the opposite of what we want).

Digest schema (`ticket_digest`): `{problem_keywords: string[], component_or_area: string, key_entities: string[], propositions: string[]}`; `propositions` = 2â€“6 atomic problem/repro statements; logs/stack-traces/code discarded by the prompt. Deterministic payload (no timestamps/uuids) so the stored record is stable modulo LLM wording.

## Config-bound threading (resolves the zero-arg-builder gap)
The proposition bound is NOT enforced through the `register_contract` schema builder (it is a zero-arg `Callable[[], type]` that cannot see config). Instead: (a) the prompt instructs the model to emit MIN..MAX propositions, and (b) the op post-validates the parsed count: if the model returns MORE than max, truncate to max; if FEWER than min, keep them and set a `low_proposition_count` flag on the result (never raise). Bounds live on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`, env `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN/MAX`, read in `LLMConfig.from_env`) â€” the LLM-tunable layer; `_config_schema.py` reserves `llm.*` so these must NOT go on `VerifyConfig`.

## Scope (exact edit targets)
- Config field target: `src/rebar/llm/config.py` (`LLMConfig.overlap_propositions_min/max`, `from_env`).
- New op module `src/rebar/llm/enrich.py`; export `enrich` from `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` (NOT `src/rebar/__init__.py`; core must not import `rebar.llm`).
- Prompt `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md`, front-matter `execution_mode: single_turn`, `outputs: ticket_digest`, a `category` value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member) (valid execution_mode enum is `single_turn|agentic` per `prompts.py:74`; `1-TURN` is invalid).
- Output schema `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_digest.schema.json` (field-level: all four fields required; arrays of non-empty strings; `propositions` minItems 1); register in `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` via `register_contract("ticket_digest", <builder>)` â€” the builder lives beside the op in `enrich.py`.
- `LLMConfig` (`src/rebar/llm/config.py`): add `overlap_propositions_min` (default 2) / `overlap_propositions_max` (default 6) to the `from_env` key set.

## Dependencies
None (leaf). S2/S5/S7 consume this op + the `ticket_digest` schema (they are sibling stories; the grep-the-codebase reviewer will not see them until they land).

## Testing
- Unit `tests/unit/test_enrich.py`: `test_schema_valid` (payload validates), `test_logs_excluded` (stack-trace frames absent from digest), `test_no_nondeterministic_fields`, `test_propositions_bounded` (>max truncated to max; <min sets low_proposition_count flag, no raise), `test_bad_json` (FakeRunner malformed â†’ clean error, no partial write), `test_absent_llm` (raises cleanly).
- Quality check as a PYTEST FIXTURE test (NOT the prompt-eval harness, which needs a registered deterministic scorer that does not exist for the `ticket_digest` shape): `test_enrich_quality` over â‰Ą6 independently-labeled tickets (fixtures at `tests/fixtures/enrich_quality/`, incl. a ~4000-token body) asserts the digest's `problem_keywords`+`propositions` name the core problem and â‰Ą1 correct entity. Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py -q`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` returns a `ticket_digest`-valid payload; `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_schema_valid`.
- [ ] Logs/stack-traces/code excluded; `::test_logs_excluded`.
- [ ] Payload has no nondeterministic fields; `::test_no_nondeterministic_fields`.
- [ ] `propositions` count enforced in the op (>max truncate, <min flag, no raise); `::test_propositions_bounded`.
- [ ] Malformed structured output â†’ tolerant-parse clean error, no partial write; `::test_bad_json`. Absent-LLM â†’ clean raise; `::test_absent_llm`.
- [ ] Quality fixture test `test_enrich_quality` (â‰Ą6 independently-labeled tickets) asserts the digest names the core problem + â‰Ą1 correct entity; `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_enrich_quality`.
